entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
Hello,

 

thank you for a very good explanation.

Nevertheless, I've implemented a new check rule with anew check step in BADI UKM_CHECK_STEP. But it's not called, at least I can't stop in debugger in Credit limit check simulation. Also  even in standard steps like UKM_CHECK_010, UKM_CHECK_130 I failed to set a break-point. It's not stopped in simulation. Could give a tip, how to debug?

Best regards,

Janna

entry.title: Re: Document Due Notification
entry.content:
Hy

Please refer to KBA 2087467 regarding Document Due and Late notifications, which states that these notifications are sent to the owner of the respective workflow step.

Thanks

entry.title: Digitale Adoption mit WalkMe in der Praxis: Erfahrungen der Deutschen Telekom Einführung in Digital
entry.content:
Die Community Session der SAP Change und Training Community bot am 22.05. einen Einblick in die Nutzung von Digital Adoption Plattformen, insbesondere WalkMe. Laura Hartmann und Dennis Buchholz von der Deutschen Telekom stellten ihre Erfahrungen mit WalkMe vor.

Was ist WalkMe?

Nach einem kurzen Checkin startete Laura und ging zunächst auf WalkMe ein:

WalkMe ist eine Digital Adoption Plattform (DAP), die wie eine interaktive Glasscheibe über jede Software-Anwendung gelegt werden kann und Nutzer:innen durch kontextsensitive Hilfen, Schritt-für-Schritt-Anleitungen und automatisierte Workflows hilft, komplexe Systeme effizienter zu nutzen. Die Plattform ist einem GPS-System ähnlich, das den Weg von A nach B weist. Die Plattform erkennt, wo sich der Nutzer:innen befinden und bietet kontextbezogene Hilfestellung. Zudem gibt es Reporting zum Status der User Adoption und verschiedene Möglichkeiten der Automation. Laura erklärte zunächst den Ansatz der Telekom rund um digital Adoption.

Der Lean Adoption Ansatz der Telekom

Die Telekom MMS verwendet einen strukturierten Ansatz für Digital Adoption Projekte, der vom Lean Startup-Konzept inspiriert wurde. Dieser "Lean Adoption"-Prozess besteht aus folgenden Phasen und Aktionen:

Ergänzt wird dieser Prozess durch zwei wichtige Komponenten:

  • Enablement auf Kundenseite
  • Aufbau eines Centers of Excellence für den unternehmensweiten Austausch

Erfolgsfaktoren für Digital Adoption Projekte

Die Telekom hat in sechs Jahren WalkMe-Implementierungen wesentliche Erfolgsfaktoren identifiziert:

Orientierung entlang der User Journey statt purer Tool-Fokus

  • Mailings vor Go-live an Stakeholder und Mitarbeitende
  • Intranet-Beiträge und User Group Meetings
  • WalkMe Days als Bootcamp-Format für den Austausch
  • Videobotschaften von Führungskräften zur Unterstützung

 Klare Messbare Ziele, KPIs und Nutzen

  • Klare Definition von KPIs und Business Objectives
  • Nachverfolgung der Nutzungsdaten
  • Regelmäßige Insight Sessions nach Implementierung
  • Kontinuierliche Anpassung an Nutzerverhalten

Berücksichtigung des Reifegrads der Organisation und klare Verantwortungen

  • Start mit kleinen Projekten und Success Stories
  • Interne Bewerbung der Erfolge
  • Berücksichtigung der Fehlerkultur
  • Klare Zuständigkeiten und Aufbau eines Centers of Excellence
  • Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs)

WalkMe Praxisbeispiele aus und in der Telekom

Die Telekom setzt WalkMe in ganz verschiedenen Bereichen ein:

Salesforce CRM

WalkMe unterstützt den Vertrieb beim Wechsel auf die neue CRM-Plattform durch In-App-Guides und Onboarding, reduziert Schulungsaufwand und fördert Akzeptanz.  

Intranet (YaM)

 

WalkMe ermöglichte Self-Service im Intranet in 6 Sprachen – vom Onboarding bis zur Hilfestellung für die Seitenerstellung.

Ticketing-Tool SKSWMS

 

WalkMe erleichtert Hotline-Mitarbeitenden die Ticketbearbeitung mit kontextuellen Anleitungen & Automatisierungen – für schnellere Bearbeitung und weniger Fehler.  ROI von über 300% bei 400 Nutzern.

Prozessautomatisierung

 

WalkMe spart produktive Minuten durch Prozessautomatisierung in 8 Anwendungen innerhalb der DT ISP.  

Konkreter Einsatz von WalkMe im Ticketing System

Dennis Buchholz demonstrierte anhand des Ticketing-Systems SKSWMS verschiedene WalkMe-Komponenten.

Die Mitarbeitenden der Telekom Hotline standen vor einer großen Herausforderung in ihrem Arbeitsalltag. Sie mussten mit einem veralteten und komplexen System arbeiten, ständig zwischen verschiedenen Anwendungen wechseln und benötigten umfangreiches Fachwissen, um auf unterschiedliche Kundenanliegen schnell reagieren zu können.

Besonders betroffen waren 400 Hotline-Mitarbeitende, deren Arbeit durch eine besonders hohe Komplexität gekennzeichnet war.

Um diese Situation zu verbessern, wurden verschiedene WalkMe-Lösungen eingesetzt. Direkt im System wurden Schritt-für-Schritt-Hilfen implementiert, die die Mitarbeitenden durch die Prozesse führten.

Wiederkehrende Aufgaben wurden durch Automatisierungen stark vereinfacht. Für schnelle Hilfestellung sorgten "Smart Tipps" und gezielte Hinweise an den richtigen Stellen. Besonders wirksam war ein Chatbot mit API-Anbindung, der direkte Systemaktionen ausführen konnte.

Die Ergebnisse dieser Maßnahmen waren laut Dennis beeindruckend. Die Mitarbeitenden zeigten sich deutlich zufriedener, da sie ihre Aufgaben nun einfacher und korrekter bearbeiten konnten.

Das Unternehmen erzielte einen Return on Investment von über 300% durch die gezielte Entlastung der Mitarbeitenden. Allein durch die automatisierte Erstellung von Briefen konnten Kosten im fünfstelligen Bereich eingespart werden. Diese Implementierung zeigt, wie digitale Adoption-Lösungen den Arbeitsalltag spürbar verbessern und gleichzeitig wirtschaftliche Vorteile bringen können.

Anbei nochmals die verschiedenen Elemente im Detail, die eingesetzt wurden:

Launcher

  • Hinzufügen von Buttons zur Oberfläche
  • Zentrale Platzierung häufig genutzter Links

WalkMe Menu

  • Schneller Zugriff auf häufig genutzte Inhalte
  • Zentrale Suche für Prozessdokumentationen (Ressourcen)
  • Zugang zu Onboarding Inhalten
  • Ablageort für Smart Walkthrus und Shuttles (Verlinkungen zu anderen Webseiten oder Tools)

Smart Walk-Thrus

  • Schritt-für-Schritt-Anleitungen durch Prozesse
  • Automatisches Ausfüllen von Formularen
  • Prüfung von Rahmenbedingungen (z.B. Kontingente)

Smart Tips

  • Kontextbezogene Informationen direkt in der Anwendung
  • Vermeidung der Suche in externen Dokumentationen

Action Bot

  • Chatbot mit Aktionsfähigkeiten
  • Abfrage wichtiger Informationen
  • API-Anbindung an Unternehmensanwendungen und Daten
  • Automatisierung von Prozessen (z.B. Brieferstellung)

Technische Integration

Walk Me lässt sich vergleichsweise einfach in bestehende Systeme integrieren:

  • Registrierung im API Gateway des Unternehmens
  • Anmeldung an relevanten API-Endpunkten
  • Low-Code-Ansatz für API-Konfiguration
  • Unterstützung verschiedener Umgebungen (Test, QA, Produktion)

Inspirierende Aussagen von Laura und Dennis

Anbei einige Aussagen der beiden die bei mir hängen geblieben sind:

  • "Es reicht nicht, einfach nur ein Tool einzuführen - Menschen brauchen Orientierung entlang ihres Arbeitstages."
  • "Digital Adoption braucht immer Pain Points und einen messbaren Nutzen, um das Maximum herauszuholen."
  • "Was ich an WalkMe besonders schätze, ist die Einfachheit - wir können als Fachbereich selbst publizieren ohne IT-Beteiligung."
  • „Man kann Leute einfach dran setzen und es ist ziemlich intuitiv - man muss nicht monatelang studieren, bevor man es anwenden kann."
  • "Wir nehmen wirklich die Notwendigkeit raus, die Leute immer wieder nachzuschulen, weil wir das einfach durch WalkMe abfangen können."
  • „Wenn ich etwas zu sagen hätte und Budget hätte, hätte ich auch WalkMe gekauft 😊“

Tipps für die Praxis in Digital Adoption Projekten

Anbei eine Zusammenfassung verschiedener Tipps, die während der Session geteilt wurden. 

  1. Klar definierte Ziele setzen: Vor der Implementierung messbare KPIs und Business Objectives festlegen.
  2. Iterativ vorgehen: Früh lernen statt lange planen - kleine Erfolge erzielen und darauf aufbauen.
  3. Ressourcen für Lifecycle-Management einplanen: Ausreichend Kapazitäten für kontinuierliche Anpassung und Aktualisierung der Inhalte bereitstellen.
  4. Kommunikation priorisieren: Nutzer:innen durch gezielte Kommunikation vor, während und nach der Einführung begleiten.
  5. User-Feedback einbeziehen: Nutzungsdaten analysieren und für kontinuierliche Verbesserung nutzen.

 Laura fasste Ihre Lernerfahrungen auch nochmals in einer Folie zusammen - anbei der Screenshot davon:

 

Zusammenfassung der Q&A-Session am Ende

API-Integration

Auf die Frage zur API-Anbindung erklärte Dennis Buchholz, dass Walk Me relativ einfach über ein API Gateway angebunden werden kann. Nach einmaliger Registrierung und Erhalt der Credentials ist hauptsächlich die Anbindung an die jeweiligen Endpunkte erforderlich. Der Low-Code-Ansatz macht die Implementierung benutzerfreundlich – so dass nach kurzer Einarbeitung erste Ergebnisse erzielt werden können.

KI-Features

Bezüglich KI-Funktionalitäten wurde erwähnt, dass WalkMe bereits verschiedene Features wie eigene Assistenten und Vorschläge für die nächsten sinnvollen Aktionen bietet. Auf der SAPPHIRE- wurden weitere KI-Integrationen vorgestellt wie die Integration in SAP Joule (sehr spannend!).

Systemanforderungen und Anwendungsbereiche

WalkMe funktioniert nicht nur in Browsern, sondern auch mit Desktop-Anwendungen wie SAP ECC und auf mobilen Geräten. 

SAP SuccessFactors-Integration von WalkMe

Für SuccessFactors-Module wird WalkMe als zukunftsweisende Lösung empfohlen, besonders wenn SAP Enable Now noch nicht im Einsatz ist. Inhalte, die mit SAP Enable Now als Standardcontent bereit gestellt wurden, werden bereits in WalkMe migriert. Auch gibt es neue Standard-Inhalte in verschiedenen Lösungen wie SuccessFactors.

Wartungsaufwand 

Der technische Nachbearbeitungsaufwand ist dank des intuitiven Editors gering. WalkMe's patentierte DeepUI-Technologie erkennt automatisch HTML-Strukturen und passt sich bei Änderungen an, sodass Inhalte bei kleineren Updates nicht sofort brechen.

Langzeitnutzung & Life-Cycle Management

Die Langzeitnutzung geht deutlich über die anfängliche Einführungsphase hinaus. Dennis hat über 600 WalkMe-Elemente erstellt und pflegt diese kontinuierlich. Entscheidend ist, ausreichend Ressourcen für die laufende Wartung und Anpassung an neue Releases einzuplanen. Dies ist ein kritischer Erfolgsfaktor für digitale Adoption. 

Test- und Produktiv-Umgebungen

Für verschiedene Systemumgebungen bietet WalkMe flexible Lösungen: Man kann unterschiedliche Oberflächen für Test-, QA- und Produktivumgebungen anlegen. Bei Kunden mit nur einem Produktivsystem kann mit Segmentierungen gearbeitet werden, sodass Inhalte zunächst nur für bestimmte Nutzergruppen sichtbar sind.

Zusammenfassung & Recap

Die Implementierung von Digital Adoption Plattformen wie WalkMe erfordert mehr als nur technisches Know-how. Erfolgskritisch ist ein strukturierter Ansatz, der die Nutzer:innen in den Mittelpunkt stellt. Die Deutsche Telekom hat mit ihrem "Lean Adoption"-Ansatz einen Weg gefunden, Digital Adoption Projekte effizient und wirkungsvoll umzusetzen.

Die vorgestellten Praxisbeispiele zeigen, dass WalkMe nicht nur bei der initialen Einführung von Software wertvolle Dienste leistet, sondern auch langfristig zur Effizienzsteigerung und Prozessoptimierung beiträgt.  Wir werden das Thema aus verschiedenen Perspektiven weiter beleuchten – gewünscht war u.a. auch die Erfahrung bzgl. der Integration in SAP Systeme sowie die Bedeutung für SAP Enable Now Kunden. 

Die nächsten Erfahrungsberichte und viele weitere Inspirationen und Austausch-Möglichkeiten gibt es bereits im SAP Learning und Adoption Forum am 24/25.06.  

WalkMe bietet auch weitere Einblicke in ihrem "WalkMe on Tour Event" in Frankfurt am 11. Juni. 

Auch möchten wir Euch gerne zur nächsten virtuellen Kaffee-Ecke einladen:

Die Rolle von Führungskräften im Lernen und Change Management in SAP Projekten - 17. Juli - 15 Uhr – virtuell, mit Melanie Zucker - Director Project Management - Change | TRANS4RM Program
adidas. Melanie hat über 30 Jahre Erfahrungen bei adidas in den Bereichen Finanzen, Supply Chain, Produktentwicklung und IT, Prozess- und Veränderungsmanagement und auch großen SAP Transformationen. In der Kaffee-Ecke reflektiert sie die Rolle von Führungskräften. Einbindung, Befähigung, Alignment, Kommunikation, Ziele und Erwartungen aber auch die eigene Führungsrolle sind u.a. Themen. 

Wir freuen uns über Eure Fragen oder Kommentare hier unter dem Blog.

entry.title: Re: Field Original Asset is not ready for input
entry.content: There is a KBA on this error, read first 2883430 - AY219: Field.... is not ready for input error when uploading Fixed Asset template in the Migration Cockpit.

entry.title: Re: Israel VAT Report PCN874
entry.content:
Hi,

Please refer SAP Notes:  1423215 &  1417958.

Hope above information will help you.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Requirement and Stock Segmentation for Country Of Origin
entry.content:
Hello Ankuagra,

nicely written, I red a E-bitte, but did not find if Segmentation is activated, can be later deactivated (reversed)? From 40 back to 16 characters?

 

Best regards,

Marko

entry.title: Re: Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal
entry.content:
Hi Bob,

This applies to all our web UI technologies, in other words also SAP GUI for HTML. It does not apply to SAP GUI for Windows, which continues to support many themes, even far older ones.

Regards,

  Tom

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
Hello @janna_kor ,

External breakpoint should stop. Make sure it is set for the same user who trigger the credit check.
Session breakpoints are not stopping, because the credit check is technically called in an other session.

Best regards,
Edina

entry.title: Re: No change log for field EINE-AUT_SOURCE in ME14 or CDPOS
entry.content:
Check if the data element (should also be AUT_SOURCE) has the flag 'Change Document' set in your system.

  • If the flag is already set, look for OSS notes, and if none relevant note found, raise a customer case.

entry.title: Re: Sales order header text to billing doc. header text n...
entry.content:
Hi ,

thanks for reply.

Below you have header level for the 

Next we have ( billing instruction is the text that is not copied )

 

 

for Item we have:

 

 

What makes me wonder is fact that after trying to create billng document from sales order I can see info that text could be obtained

 

but its not copied 😞

moreover in item log view access sequence is 560 not 550

 

thank you

entry.title: Re: Adding Pricing Conditions to an Existing Purchasing I...
entry.content:
Hi, i tried the same API and the same body in PATCH mode

PATCH https://{{host}}:443/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_INFORECORD_PROCESS_SRV/A_PurgInfoRecdOrgPlantData(PurchasingInfoRecord='5300000070',PurchasingInfoRecordCategory='0',PurchasingOrganization='2310',Plant='2310')/to_PurInfoRecdPrcgCndnValidity

and body

{
    "ConditionRecord": "0000007208",
    "ConditionValidityEndDate": "/Date(1492041600000)/",
    "ConditionValidityStartDate": "/Date(1492041600000)/",
    "ConditionApplication": "M",
    "ConditionType": "PPR0",
    "PurchasingOrganization": "2310",
    "PurchasingInfoRecordCategory": "0",
    "PurchasingInfoRecord": "5300000070",
    "Supplier": "1000000",
    "Material": "ZTEC_ACQ1",
    "Plant": "2310"
}

but i receive error "The specified HTTP method is not allowed for the resource identified by the Data Service Request URI"

Can you help me?

Riccardo

entry.title: Re: IBP response production orders
entry.content: Can you please re share that note? I can't see it and I have the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Can anyone link me to Hana DB SQL queries documentati...
entry.content:
Hi,

HANA expressions guide can be found on this link:

Expressions 

You can see on the left side separate sections for functions and statements and how to use them.

As for formatted search, complete guide can be found here.

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: Multiple Item Valuation Method used into Items SAP Bu...
entry.content:
Hi,

The valuation method is key to evaluating inventory value and affects a company's financial reports. It’s best to get advice from the financial team on this matter.

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: Re: Sales order header text to billing doc. header text n...
entry.content:
Did you use Transaction VOTXN or SPRO: Sales and Distribution > Basic Functions > Text Control > Define Text Types to

  1. Configure Text Determination
    Define Text Types (Text IDs) for Sales Order and Billing Document.
    Assign Text Types to Text Procedures: Header level of Sales Order and Billing
    Assign Text Procedures to Document Types: E.g., OR for sales order, F2 for billing
  2. Set Up Text Copying Rules
    Use "Access Sequence" to define how text should be copied.
    Under "Text Determination Procedure Assignment":
    Maintain copying rules from Sales Order to Billing.

entry.title: Re: Birthday/Anniversary notifications with an image through Intelligent Services
entry.content:
Hi Sachin,
I have used below content in the alert message that is pointing to a sharepoint. Image access is granted to all from sharepoint. Yet image does not load in the birthday mail received. Can you please suggest

<img src="https://finolexind-my.sharepoint.com/:i:/r/personal/5512_finolexind_com/Documents/Birthday%20Mailers/Happy%20Birthday%20(1).jpg" width=700" height="750"" >

Thanks,
Ram

entry.title: Re: IBP response production orders
entry.content:
Hi Carlos,

 

Could you find a solution?

entry.title: Question about SAP Business One Additional Expense
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently using the SAP Business One, whose database is SAP HANA. The version is 10.00.120 PL:02 (64bit).

I am trying to add an Additional Expense to Delivery/Sales Order/Inventory Item, but I can't find where it is. I found some articles said that it is at:

Administration > System Initialization > Document Settings > General Tab. Check the "Manage Expenses in Documents" check box. Once it is checked, a "Define Expenses" button is shown. Click the button to define expenes.

But I can't find it, here is my UI:

As you see, the only one may be a little related to this is "Manage Freight in Documents".

So, my questions are:

1. How to create an Additional Expense?

2.Once I created it, how to set it to the Delivery or Sales Order or Inventory Item?

Could you give me some advice? Thank you in advance!

entry.title: SuccessFactors Document Management and Time Management - 2 events
entry.content:
Mastering time: smarter workforce management with SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking. Co-organised with SAP and Pentos with Erik Ebert May 28 2025. 

https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/6d7879fd-ea4f-4725-bf8b-896b4585075f@ac33bb23-bea2-4ff5-bd61-f103925fe989

Taming the paper chaos: HR Document Management in SuccessFactors and when to choose a partner solution like Centric. Co-organised between Centric and Pentos with Erik Ebert. June 11 2025 

https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/48b8ed3f-8906-49ea-8ed9-572ebbf4136e@ac33bb23-bea2-4ff5-bd61-f103925fe989

SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking

entry.title: Re: Problem for creation of cost element category 31
entry.content:
Hello Steffen, the solution suggested above with T-code OB_GLACC14 works fine. Let me just provide some additional information.

The new GL account (as Secondary Cost Element) need to be created in FSP0. Then with OB_GLACC14 (Mass change), you select your Controlling Area and you add your Cost Element Category 31.

This Cost Element will appear in FS00 for all Company Code in the Controlling Area without being link to any. Nice trick.

Then you can use in T-code OKG2 for WIP or C+VF configuration. This Cost Element will be accepted as "Technical RA Cost Element".

Hope it will help other to save time.

entry.title: Re: No change log for field EINE-AUT_SOURCE in ME14 or CDPOS
entry.content: Thank you, the flag was indeed missing !

entry.title: Re: Gender Dimension problem
entry.content: Hi YSE! Thank you, yes this was the solution. I still get a few unallocated records, around 20 people from the 17k employees that we have, but it's ok. The dashboard looks much better now. Ok, so everywhere I'm using Person ID it needs to be setup as Secondary Person and User Sys ID as Primary Person. Got it! In the manual implementation guide, it's the other way around just to let you know.

entry.title: Strengthening Your SAP CPQ Deployment with Continuous Quality Checks
entry.content:
Are you implementing SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) or managing its day-to-day operations? In both scenarios, ensuring the quality and stability of your solution is vital. SAP Enterprise Support offers continuous quality checks to help you achieve this. Continuous quality checks are included as part of your SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition maintenance contract, embedded in your SAP CPQ subscription at no extra cost.

SAP CPQ is a cloud solution that enables businesses to streamline and optimize their sales processes. It allows sales teams to accurately configure complex products, generate error-free quotes, and automate approvals, all while ensuring compliance with business rules and improving overall sales efficiency. It can empower your sales team and partners to offer complex product configurations, optimized pricing, and great-looking proposals. 

Below is the continuous quality check (CQC) available to strengthen your SAP CPQ deployment, tailored to your specific implementation phase and needs:

 

Service

Benefit

When to request

CQC for Deployment Readiness for SAP Configure, Price, Quote (CPQ)

Ensures operational efficiency and readiness by performing a detailed analysis of your SAP CPQ, either before go-live or as a "health check" for your productive systems.

(Highly recommended) 
Realize phase - Two months prior to go live 

(Proactive) Run phase 

Additional Resources and Support:

To learn more about SAP CPQ onboarding and introduction, visit the e-learnings in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition value map*.

*To access the link and for ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to join and explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. 

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps 

Beyond CQC services, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription. Get started today: 

  • One-time sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using a valid S-User ID (please allow up to 2 hours for processing) 
  • Access SAP Enterprise Support value maps for personalized guidance 
  • Request a call with our solution area experts for tailored advice 

Value Maps are part of SAP Enterprise Support commitment to your success. Visit the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page to explore our range of topics and start engaging with us today. 

 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author: 

  • Icy Huang is a Focus Advisor for SAP Enterprise Support value maps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Based in Singapore, Icy has been with SAP since 2007, taking on diverse roles that have largely centered around customer engagement and success across the organization. Now she focuses on helping business accelerate the adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud solutions with value map content and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

Have questions, comments or feedback? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @IcyHuang

entry.title: Re: Configuration ID "AGS_USER_BATCH" at TAC SOLMAN_SETUP...
entry.content: Hello Vera Jiang, Thank you very much for right solution. Issue is resolved now.

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi @poojarychetan 

I'm not an integration expert, so the question isn't in my area of expertise. I checked the Business Accelerator Hub for APIs for application jobs but was unsuccessful. But that does not mean it's not possible. I would reach out to someone more on the integration side to ask this.

BR

Melanie

entry.title: Re: How-to-guide for SAP GUI Scripting
entry.content: We have a customer case wherein the Macros enabled excel is not working in WEB GUI but it is only working in SAP GUI. IS there a fix to this issue?

entry.title: OData response time reduced significantly after model changes and fastens up after 8-9 hours
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing a strange issue.

I have built an unmanaged RAP application with 8 entities in SAP on-prem system where all of them are related to each other via associations and compositions. Each entity on an average are having around 25-30 fields. I have created cds, behavior, metadata extensions, service definition and OData V2 UI service binding.

All works good, but the issue is, whenever I change any thing in my model like field logic, or add additional field etc., OData response slows down significantly.

It takes around 35 - 40 seconds to load metadata sometimes it takes even minutes. It again fastens up after 8 - 9 hours and loads in 600 - 800ms.

I tried doing everything including

invalidating global cache

invalidating client cache

invalidating metadata cache

I even tried running report /UI5/UPD_ODATA_METADATA_CACHE

Nothing helps

I would be happy to know what is the potential cause for this and how to resolve

 

metadata loads in 670ms without any modifications.

 

Same service taking 35 seconds after field modification

entry.title: SAP Business One Create Invoice using SL but got a NotFound error
entry.content:
Hello, 

We have made a software that uses ServiceLayer to create Invoice from Delivery or SalesOrder. But now, a new customer reported that he got a NotFound error when trying to create an Invoice from Delivery, and here is the http request:

POST https://example.com/sl/b1s/v1/Invoices

Content is:

{
  "CardCode": "C00001",
  "DocEntry": 0,
  "DocObjectCode": 0,
  "DocNum": 0,
  "TrackingNumber": "123456789123",
  "PickRemark": "",
  "DocumentLines": [
    {
      "BaseEntry": 33,
      "LineNum": 0,
      "BaseLine": 0,
      "BaseType": 15,
      "WarehouseCode": "02",
      "ItemCode": "012.34.A-B",
      "Quantity": 1.0,
      "RemainingOpenQuantity": 0.0,
      "UoMCode": "",
      "CommodityClassification": 0,
      "CountryOrg": "",
      "BatchNumbers": [],
      "SerialNumbers": [],
      "TaxCode": "TAXON",
      "Price": 0.0
    }
  ],
  "DocumentAdditionalExpenses": [
    {
      "LineNum": 0,
      "ExpenseCode": 5,
      "LineTotal": 15.98,
      "Remarks": "// SomeExpense Canada Post Expedited Parcel",
      "TaxCode": "TAXON",
      "BaseDocEntry": null,
      "BaseDocLine": null,
      "BaseDocType": null
    }
  ],
  "DocumentPackages": [
    {
      "Number": 1,
      "DocumentPackageItems": [
        {
          "ItemCode": "012.34.A-B",
          "Quantity": 1.0,
          "MeasureUnit": "",
          "UoMEntry": -1,
          "UnitsOfMeasurement": 1.0
        }
      ],
      "Type": "Box"
    }
  ],
  "DownPaymentsToDraw": [],
  "B1S_ReplaceCollectionsOnPatch": false,
  "U_V33SB_ShopifyOrderId": "123456789",
  "U_V33SB_ShopifyRefundId": "",
  "U_V33SB_TrackingUpdated": "N",
  "U_V33SB_ShopifyFulfillmentId": "",
  "U_V33_BranchName": "ABCDEFG",
  "U_IdocsComments": "",
  "U_V33SB_EXTERNALORDERID": "",
  "U_UpdateIDocs": "",
  "U_IsInvoiceFullyPaid": "",
  "U_V33_StarShipFreight": "15.98"
}

The error is No matching record found (ODBC -2028).

But they do have an expense whose Expenses Code is 5:

{
	"Name" : "ShopifyDifference",
	"RevenuesAccount" : "_SYS00000000834",
	"ExpenseAccount" : "_SYS00000000834",
	"TaxLiable" : "tNO",
	"FixedAmountRevenues" : 0.0,
	"FixedAmountExpenses" : 0.0,
	"OutputVATGroup" : null,
	"InputVATGroup" : null,
	"DistributionMethod" : "aed_None",
	"Includein1099" : "tNO",
	"FreightOffsetAccount" : null,
	"WTLiable" : "N",
	"ExpensCode" : 5,
	"ExpenseExemptedAccount" : null,
	"RevenuesExemptedAccount" : null,
	"DistributionRule" : "",
	"DrawingMethod" : "dmTotal",
	"FreightType" : "ftShipping",
	"Stock" : "tNO",
	"LastPurchasePrice" : "tNO",
	"Project" : null,
	"DistributionRule2" : "",
	"DistributionRule3" : "",
	"DistributionRule4" : "",
	"DistributionRule5" : "",
	"DataVersion" : 1,
	"SAFTProductType" : "saftpt_Services"
}

But it seems I don't set the Additional Expense properly in my environment (Maybe you can give some help about this).

Could you give me some help about this NotFound problem? Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Hi Team,Open GR/IR balance migration tools in S/4 Han...
entry.content:
Thank you.. Actually we are using DMC toll to migrate Open PO’s,

But client looking to migrate PO with full Qty where ever GR/IR pending.

DMC toll will supports PO history migration aswell ? (Example: Post bulk Goods receipt without impacting Stock GL’s).

We didn't found Object in DMC for PO history migration.. Please suggest if any tool supports my requirement in  S/4 Hana environment..

Our FI team not interested to perform Manual GR/IR clearance,  

Note: Regarding 2 Question answer without impacting stock posting, Specific GL need to assign in OBYC BSX or Direct in PO w.r.t AA. Pls confirm

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere - product FAQ
entry.content:
Hi @Rick_L 

Yes, this would be an option in the future. Our Data Export Service shall support hierarchies starting HY2 2025 (not on roadmap yet). 

What I did for demos (and it was a workaround but pretty straight forward) was to mirror the parent child hierarchy information into a standard property on the dimension which I then assigned as parent in Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP: Automatically add row to table when another...
entry.content:
Hi, I checked and am able to execute the factory action during creation of child entity A but I have a problem implementing it. Both children entities are defined in the behavior definition as "lock dependent by _rootEntity" and when i want use MODIFY ENTITY root CREATE BY \childB in the factory action I always get failed and I can see it is because the dependency of locking entities...

Screenshot of failed
root entity



childB



is there any other way to do it?
I was able to do an insert but then - I do not see these records for childB in table immediately (only after refresh) so i do not want to have it working like that.

entry.title: Re: How to clear parameter after clicking selective radio...
entry.content:
You are a lifesaver. I used your technique, and it works. Below is my enhanced code: I have added IF...ELSE condition just after the LOOP AT SCREEN function.

Now it is user-friendly, as we know, business users sometimes make mistakes, and it becomes a hefty problem for developers. So this way I can approach real-time projects.

entry.title: Re: Unable to add discount condition type to the purchase...
entry.content: Your first screen shot is from condition record. Here the combination depends on the access sequence (fields) configured. In the second case, it is from PO condition tab and not from condition record. Compare with same transaction code for any issue

entry.title: Re: HeaderInfo - Transalate or change Text 'Object Inform...
entry.content:
💡 Fiori Elements V4 :

If you want to hide the Header Content section in an Object Page, just add the following setting to your manifest.json:

"editableHeaderContent": false

This disables editing for the header area and effectively hides it from the UI.
Simple, clean, and effective! 🚀

Sample Code (Source)
 
"sap.ui5": {
        ...
        "routing": {
                ...
                {
                    "pattern": "SalesOrderManage({key})/_Item({key2}):?query:",
                    "name": "SalesOrderItemObjectPage",
                    "target": "SalesOrderItemObjectPage"
                }
            ],
            "targets": {
                "SalesOrderItemObjectPage": {
                    "type": "Component",
                    "id": "SalesOrderItemObjectPage",
                    "name": "sap.fe.templates.ObjectPage",
                    "options": {
                        "settings": {
                            "contextPath": "/SalesOrderItem",
                            "editableHeaderContent": false,
                            ...
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

entry.title: Re: SAP ME- meint queue error -"Operation XXX does not ex...
entry.content:
Hi!

Check if the operation has a Current Version selected.

Check if the routing includes all steps from ERP or mixing them with ME-specific steps.

If none of those, please submit a support ticket to MFG-ME-INT component.

Regards,

Sergiy

entry.title: Re: Padding is invalid and cannot be removed
entry.content: If you're encountering the error “Padding is invalid and cannot be removed” on the server but not locally, and it’s happening while working with the Crystal Report file you uploaded (GeneralLedgerDetail.rpt), the issue is most likely related to encryption/decryption in your server-side application, not the report design itself.

entry.title: Calling a Remote Action from Event Handler in CAP Java
entry.content:
Hello together,

does anyone have an example or guidance on how to call a remote action from an event handler in CAP Java?

In Node.js, calling a remote action is quite straightforward, as shown in the official documentation:
👉 https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/core-services#consuming-services

However, in Java, I understand that it involves working with CQN and using the remote service proxy:
👉 https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/cqn-services/remote-services

Unfortunately, I haven’t found a concrete example yet. For instance, the SAP Cloud CAP Java samples don't seem to demonstrate calling a remote action specifically.

Has anyone implemented this or could share a snippet or an approach for calling a remote-bound action from within a CAP Java event handler?

Thanks a lot in advance and best regards,

Julian

Hi @kansara81 Apologies for my late reply. Please can you check if there was anything maintained for client 100 in following IMG path:

If so that might explain the different behaviour. In case you can find a query, then feel free to take over that customising to your client 300.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: Close Group Tree button is hidden in Crystal report v...

entry.title: Re: Resize of Group Tree does not work anymore
entry.content:
Hi Charles,

Thank you for posting, the issue has already been reported and should be fixed in SP 38.

See this post for details:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/close-group-tree-button-is-hidden-in-crystal-report-viewer-after-upgrading/qaq-p/14015234/comment-id/4903289#M4903289

Don

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Workflow, Situation Handling, KPIs,
entry.content:
@StephanHeinberg Yes...  well observed... and there are good reasons for that...

1. With SAP S/4HANA we try to smooth the journey where we can in.  To minimize disruption, existing classic workflows are still supported in a system conversion scenario. 

2. My Inbox is one of very few SAP Fiori apps that can be used in both ECC and SAP S/4HANA.

3. Many of the Flexible Workflows that were written for SAP S/4HANA provide newly designed sub-tasks that work with My Inbox, support mobile devices, and lift the UI design overall. These can also be federated up to SAP Task Center.

4. SAP Task Center is considered the go-to unified inbox across multiple SAP Cloud solutions, and is definitely worth considering.

Creating Custom Fields | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: How to Enable Auto-Approval for Leaves of Three Days or Less
entry.content:
We need to configure different leave approval workflows in SuccessFactors based on employee levels. Additionally, we’d like to explore the possibility of auto-approving leave requests that are up to three consecutive days, without requiring manual approval. Managers would still be notified of such leaves.

Could you please confirm if this configuration is feasible in SuccessFactors? Also, what potential challenges or limitations should we be aware of when implementing this setup?

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements on CDS: filter values not visible
entry.content: I am facing the same issue. How did you resolve this?

entry.title: Re: Can anyone link me to Hana DB SQL queries documentati...
entry.content:
SQL dialect differs between different databases of different vendors. SAP HANA's compliance with the mandatory features of the ANSI SQL/2016 standard: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4fe29514fd584807ac9f2a04f6754767/09940a1ed38c4ceb80770632997b286b.html?locale=en-US

In SAP HANA SQL, the DUMMY table is a special one-row, one-column table that is often used when a query requires a FROM clause but no actual table data is needed. It is similar to the DUAL table in Oracle.

Here are some common use cases for the DUMMY table:

  1. Selecting a Constant Value: When you want to return a constant value or perform a calculation that doesn't involve any table data, you can use the DUMMY table.

     
     
SELECT 'Hello, World!' AS greeting FROM DUMMY;

  • Calling Functions: When you need to call a function that doesn't require any table data.

     
     
SELECT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS now FROM DUMMY;

entry.title: Re: Functional Area Hierarchy in Group Financial Statemen...

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
20190422_SAP NOW_S4 Move_Faycal Chraibi

Moving To S/4HANA Options and Customer Reference Template

How to move to S/4HANA based upon the most beneficial scenario for the Customer

 

SAP S/4HANA Transition Net 5 Guiding Questions

 

Move to S/4HANA Customer Reference Template - this is a really useful picture:

 

SAP S/4HANA Transition Scenarios Best Practices, this was written in 2017, but most of the principles and teachings still apply today...

The Path to S/4HANA: Transition Scenarios and Best Practices

First let's have a look at slide number 15.....

 

Actual Findings from Customer Projects and Recommendations

 

Functional Scope: Illustrative evolution of S/4HANA overtaking ECC 6.0 Functional Scope

 

Transition to S/4HANA

 

S/4HANA Family and Transition Paths

 

Transition to S/4HANA System Conversion

 

SAP S/4HANA System Conversion Overview of Process Steps

 

Recommended Preparation for a System Conversion Project

 

Transition to S/4HANA New Implementation

 

Transition to S/4HANA Landscape Transformation

 

Transition to S/4HANA Lessons Learned from the RIG Regional Implementation Group

Microsoft PowerPoint - S4HANA RIG @ User Groups.pptx

 

 

 

Transformation Plan 1.0 - Outcome summary of SAP S/4HANA Adoption Starter

Transformation Plan 1.0 Example Customer GmbH
Summary of SAP S/4HANA Adoption Starter Engagement

This is a golden document.

entry.title: How to activate Lean Catalog in SAP ECC?
entry.content:
Hi,

We are looking to use Lean Catalog for importing catalog data from suppliers into our SAP ECC and have some questions about the required steps and the functionality we can achieve.

Is Lean Catalog still a consulting add-on? If so, how do get to activate it?

Is Lean Catalog subject to separate licensing?

Are there any prerequisite SAP components that we need to have installed, like SRM?

From the documentation I can find, it looks as if Lean Catalog in current version can "survive" an upgrade to S/4HANA on premise. Is that correct?

Regards

Bodil Vestergaard

entry.title: S4 Hana on premise and BP 41V - Presales
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

we activated BP 41V in our S4 Hana on premise system and I have problem when creating folow-up documents for an opportunity. According to the script I should be able to create Sales Quotation (QT) as a follow-up document for Opportunity (OPPT). But when I click on icon for Follow-up I see only Activity types (see the attachment), Quotation is missing. Customizing is updated based on the Set-up document for 41V. Any advice how can I add additonal document types to the list of Follow-up documents?

Thanks 

Peter

entry.title: Promotion management using java SDK
entry.content:
Hey, I want to automate process of Promotion of Jobs using java SDK, I'm using 4.3 version, while using export Scenario to save file in my local directory 

package pm;
import com.crystaldecisions.sdk.exception.SDKException;
import com.crystaldecisions.sdk.framework.*;
import com.crystaldecisions.sdk.occa.infostore.*;
import com.businessobjects.lcm.job.IJob;
import com.businessobjects.lcm.utilities.LCMException;
import com.businessobjects.sdk.lcm.*;
import java.util.*;
public class ExportAndPersistJob {
    static String myCMS = "myCMS";
static String sourceCMS = "mySource";
// static String destinationCMS = "";
static String authType = "secEnterprise";
static String userName = "username";
static String password = "password";
    public static void main(String[] args) throws SDKException, LCMException {
        // Log on to the CMS
        ISessionMgr sessionMgr = CrystalEnterprise.getSessionMgr();
        IEnterpriseSession session = sessionMgr.logon(userName, password, myCMS, authType);
        session.setLocale(Locale.ENGLISH);
        // Create new host (job)
        Host host = HostFactory.newInstance(session);
        ExportScenario exportScenario = host.newExportScenario("SHRE_12testjob");
        
        // Log on to source CMS (could be same as host CMS)
        IEnterpriseSession sourceSession = sessionMgr.logon(userName, password, myCMS, authType);
        System.out.println("ran till here");
        LcmArchive exportArchive = LcmArchiveFactory.newInstance("C:\\Users\\test.lcmbiar");
        System.out.println("ran till here");
        exportScenario.setDestination(exportArchive);    
        exportScenario.setSource(session);
        String query = "SELECT * FROM CI_APPOBJECTS WHERE SI_CUID='sample'";
        ObjectBrowser browser = exportScenario.setSource(session);
        browser.addQuery(query);
        browser.computeDependencies(); 
        for (Set<IInfoObject> dependencySet : browser.getDependencies().values()) {
            browser.addObjects(dependencySet);
        }
        String result = exportScenario.schedule(password); 
        host.release();
        System.out.println("Job scheduled with ID: " + result);
        session.logoff();
        session.logoff();
    }
}
Ouput i got in terminal is 
Output is 

Logging on to the central CMS ...

Logged in to central as

Running housekeeping ...

Housekeeping complete

Creating job "SHRE_12testjob" ...

Job created

ran till here

ran till here

Logging on to the source CMS "" ...

Logged in to source as ""

Logging on to the source CMS ...

Logged in to source as

Running query on browser ...

Query returned 1 object(s)

Added 1 resource(s)

Computing dependencies ...

Dependencies computed

Added 1 resource(s)

Deleting job "SHRE_12testjob" ...

Job deleted

Saving job "SHRE_12testjob" ...

Job saved

Job "SHRE_12testjob" has been scheduled (Now)

Job scheduled with ID:

My doubt is why lcmbiar file is not exported to directory i gave? also why job is deleted?

entry.title: SAP Build Apps Decimal Issue
entry.content:
Dear Experts ,

Field used for price input should format after input to localized decimal number.

As the value is given as Text preferred formula function would be Format_localized_decimal.

On binding this gives the error message of “This binding requires an inversible formula”.

See attached screenshot:

Thanks,

Vijay

entry.title: BAPI to remove ship-to from business partner
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Is that any BAPI or FM to remove ship-to from the business partner?

Recently we have done upgrade S4 HANA. Earlier we use BDC to remove ship-to from the sold-to via XD02 t.code and works well. Now after upgrade we can't do via BDC. So, is that any BAPI or FM to achieve the case?

Thanks,

Dhina.

entry.title: Need confirmation for OS upgrade
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently planning an operating system upgrade from SUSE Linux Enterprise Server (SLES) 15 SP5 to SLES 15 SP6

  • Please let us know if the PO systems (Netweaver 7.5 SP29) and solution manager(7.2 SP14) version support the Linux version or not. 
  • Please suggest exactly where and what all we need to check for the compatibility

Regards,

Bhushan

entry.title: SAP B1 HANA approval using queries
entry.content:
Can anyone please guide me through approval stages in sap b1.I am using HANA dB.Currently I am implementing approval for purchase request.And I have udf field included in header table with valid values:PO and Good Issue.Currently if we link one approval stages with template(with 4 authorizers),it defaults to parallel approval where the approval document is sent to every users included in authorizers.So to simulate sequential authorization in such a way that only after first user approve,it will be forwarded to second approver based on value in Udf I have mentioned already,I have created stages each involving one approver and linked them all to one approval template.It should be like if value is PO,the approval should activate but if it is Good Issue,no approval needed.Here is my HANA approval query SELECT DISTINCT 'TRUE'

FROM DUMMY T0

WHERE $[OPRQ.U_PR_TYPE] = 'PO'.

Now the problem is it works for first approver ,but subsequent approval for approver 2,3 and 4 does not work.Can anyone help me how to get this right

entry.title: Re: Building a custom Digital Manufacturing Cloud POD Plugin the easy way
entry.content:
Hello Kevin, I hope this message finds you well.

I’ve been trying for a few weeks to develop a POD Plugin application. I'm stuck on a problems where I'm trying to use a plugin that consume a service in the S/4HANA. We configured a destination in the BTP connectivity services for the app we created. But we are not finding out a solution to consume that, we are allway getting errors that the destination is not founded.
I’ve tried using these resources without success: destination, Ajax, direct connection via the manifest and UI5.yaml.

We also tryed some git examples we found, but without any success. Can you help us on that?

My Best Regards,

Fernando Franco

entry.title: Re: Get technical information from Service Definition usi...
entry.content:
Hi ekalyvas,

I would say the easiest way is to send GET HTTP request to that service for metadata information:

e.g.

.../sap/opu/odata4/....zui_dasbcapaplace/0001/$metadata

In the answer there should be everything you need.

Regards

Jarda

entry.title: Re: HCM - Payroll Issue
entry.content:
Hello,

If you double-click the input table, it should show the values being submitted to rule XCM0.  It also may give you more details for the error.  It sounds like the Employee Group being passed to the rule is not being found in the rule.  But kind of hard to tell from the message.  By default, the rule is setup for Employee Groups 1, 2, 3 and a wildcard '*'.

entry.title: Re: Gender Dimension problem
entry.content: Just checked those 20 records and they seemed to be missing gender which is weird because it's mandatory. Anyways, the dimension works well. Thank you for the help.

entry.title: Stack Analytical Models
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Recently the feature to stack Analytical models (AM) has been released.


I already see two benefits:

- Data Access Control on first AM (with options to add more detailed DAC on second AM...) 

- Calculations managed on the first AM

 

I was wondering if there are other benefits/ calculations possible due to this new feature?

Would love to know how others see this feature (filtering, restrictions,..) .

 

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: EDI: Partner profile inbound not available Message no. E0337
entry.content:
Although this thread is medieval aI am posting a possible solution (cause I ran into the same issue and had to raise a case at SAP to get this resolved). I just paste the solution below:  ".....The problem is described in note 1776471 under section:

"One other very special case can be where key fields in the control record of the idoc actually matches the information in table EDP21. But the idoc still receives a status of 56. For the fields RCVPFC and SNDPFC we use in all dialogs the conversion form EDIPV (because the used domain is EDI_PARVW). On the database we don't save the value depending on the used language as there is a conversion exit behind that field so that you do not see the value that is stored on the database, but rather a language dependent one. On all dynpros you can insert 'VN' and before saving on the database this value will be converted to 'LF'. If you look at the entries then you see 'VN', but on the database you can find only 'LF'. If you send IDocs, then the fields RCVPFC and SNDPFC have to be filled with 'LF' instead of 'VN' to account for this. "....."

best regards, André

entry.title: Re: The Power of Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities
entry.content:
Hi, I am using the joule for generating Business objects from scratch (2505 update) in RAP. but it's generating the Managed Scenario only and also not generating the custom domains and elements. is there any way to generate the BO with Unmanaged Scenario? also, when I am providing the prompt with validation and custom methods for business logic, it is not creating in class. 

Note: I have updated My ADT and Eclipse to latest Version.

entry.title: Re: Get technical information from Service Definition usi...
entry.content:
Hello Jarda,

Thank you for your reply!

This is something I also though about but I would like to avoid it as I will need to parse the metadata. I was looking for a standard FM or class I could use to get the information I need rather than calling the service.

entry.title: Re: Updating material forecast
entry.content:
Hello,

same problem here - anyone knows ?

BR

Manuel

entry.title: Re: BAPI to remove ship-to from business partner
entry.content:
Hi Dhina,

i would recommend using the class CMD_EI_API and methods like MAINTAIN or MAINTAIN_BAPI.

Regards

Jarda

entry.title: Re: Functional Area Hierarchy in Group Financial Statemen...
entry.content:
Hi Tomi.

The Best Practise way (and best way in my opinion) is to use Reporting rules to prepare your P&L by Function.

This way you can combine FS items with Subitems and define the Functional P&L that way.

The resulting P&L can be presentes in both Group Data Analysis reports and Review Booklet by selecting the appropriate Reporting Hierarchy.

Br.

Jan

entry.title: Re: How to work with exemptions using ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud during development in an
entry.content:
Hi @mainak-aich,

just FYI: the point 2 is already planned in our development backlog , but it is not clear yet, when it will be developed (must be prioritized along with other requests).

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!
entry.content:
Hi, can we access the Joule from Postman or integrate it outside the SAP?

Joule SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment SAP BTP ABAP environment

entry.title: Re: I'm getting errors when attempting to install CR for...
entry.content: When installing CR for VS you must right-click and 'Run as Administrator'.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Typescript - Error - Could not find a declara...
entry.content: Thanks, npm rebuild solved it for me!

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning Hub Adoption Sessions - New webinars starting 29 May 2025
entry.content: Will there be recordings? If yes, will the link only be sent to registered participants or will it be published for everyone?

entry.title: Re: Recovery of credential from SAP INTEGRATION SUITE
entry.content:
You can take a backup. It is upto you where you want to store it and make sure back up is stored in a secured place. 

https://api.sap.com/api/SecurityContent

entry.title: Re: Sap Public Cloud - auto population of the account ass...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

There’s a BADI called MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ACCOUNT that you can implement.

Here’s some elements to be considered and to take into account :

 

Use

This Business Add-In (BAdI) is used in the Purchasing (MM-PUR-PO) component.

You can use this BAdI to make changes to fields on account assignment level.

Using the BAdI you can change the following fields:

Fieldname on the User Interface Technical Name

G/L Account Number GLACCOUNT

Cost Center COSTCENTER
Profit Center PROFITCENTER

Network Number for Account Assignment PROJECTNETWORK

Work Breakdown Structure Element (WBS Element) WBSELEMENTINTERNALID

Main Asset Number MASTERFIXEDASSET

Sales and Distribution Document Number SALESORDER

Sales Document Item SALESORDERITEM

Unloading Point UNLOADINGPOINT

Controlling Area CONTROLLINGAREA

Functional Area FUNCTIONALAREA

Service Document Type SERVICEDOCUMENTTYPE

Service Document ID SERVICEDOCUMENTID

Service Document Item ID SERVICEDOCUMENTITEMID

Goods Recipient GOODSRECIPIENTNAME

Order Number ORDERID

Note: Use the technical name of the fields for the BAdI implementation. Ensure that you do not delete standard fields unless you want to delete them. Note that SAP may add new fields and do not use statements that change the overall structure.

 

The following fields are not changeable:

Fieldname on User Interface Technical Name

Purchasing Document Number PURCHASEORDER

Item number of Purchasing Document PURCHASEORDERITEM

Sequential Number of Account Assignment ACCOUNTASSIGNMENTNUMBER

Using the BAdI, you can raise messages by filling the changing parameter table MESSAGES.

 

Standard Settings

  • The BAdI is called for each account assignment line separately.
  • The BAdI is active in the standard system supplied.

 

Activities

For information about implementing BAdIs as part of the Enhancement Concept, see SAP Library for SAP NetWeaver under BAdIs - Embedding in the Enhancement Framework.

 

Example 1

Modify account assignment goodsrecipientname:

READ TABLE purorderaccount_table INDEX 1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_accountassignment>).

READ TABLE purchaseorderitem_table INDEX 1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_pur_item>).

IF <ls_accountassignment> IS ASSIGNED AND <ls_pur_item> IS ASSIGNED.

IF <ls_pur_item>-accountassignmentcategory = 'K'.

purchaseorderaccountchange-goodsrecipientname = 'Testuser'.

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

 

Example 2

Check if accountassignmentcategory field is initial and then raise an error message. Here a non-translatable message is raised:

IF <ls_pur_item>-accountassignmentcategory = ''.

ls_message-messagetype = 'E'.

ls_message-messagevariable1 = 'The accountassignementcategory
cannot be initial'.

ls_message-messageid = 'MEPO'.

ls_message-messagenumber = '250'.

APPEND ls_message TO messages.
ENDIF.

 

I hope this will be helpful for you.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Lease asset in S4 Hana - no interest posted
entry.content:
Dear community,

 

I an trying to set up leasing for some assets for my company but I can get the interest posted.

Here is the customizing I have done : 

- Create a depreciation key LEAS with ordinary depreciation + interest

- create new screen layout with leasing spreadsheet

- create new classes for leasign and assign the depreciation key + the screen layouts

- maintain the account determination including the interest accounts

- activate interests posting in OAYR

 

When creating an asset on these new class I fill all the data in the leasing spradsheet but when I try to post the depreciation I have not interest calculated when I was expected them to be calculated using the rate fielled in field LZINS

 

Does someone have any idea on what I may have missed please ? Or have I absolutely to to configure REFX ?
